references.title: Predicting student dropout in subscription-based online learning environments: The beneficial impact of the logit leaf model

extracted.Data sources: Learner Demographics, LMS logs, Classroom characteristics
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Missing value handling depends on percentage of nans. If the number of nans is greater than 5% authors impute Zero or Missing (depending on data type).

If smaller than 5%, the students are removed

Categorical variables are One-Hot Encoded.
extracted.Models: Logit Leaf Model, Logistic Regression, SVM, CART, C4.5, Random Forest, Bagging, AdaBoost, Logistic Model Tree
extracted.Performance Metric: Best Model: {AUC: 0.845 - Logistic Regression, Top Decile Lift: 1.801 - Logistic Regression}
extracted.Comments:
Authors create a Classification Problem to Predict whether a user of an online subscription service will or not cancel his/her subscription in the next month.

Authors compare multiple algorithms and notice that, even though Logistic Regression is the “best”, there is value in using a Logit Leaf Model due to its interpretability. 

Academic Engagement is, for the most part, regarded as the most important set of variables for this prediction.
extracted.Limitations:
Limited Context  (subscription-based service)

No online testing.

annotations.text: Despite its utility, online learning is carachterized by unusually high dropout rates, which is counterintuitive to the notion of making education more accessible. Ther are systems nowadays that allow for early identification.
The paper tries to improve Dropout prediction state of the art with 3 contributions:
